title: WordCamp Spain 2010
body: [![WordCamp Spain 2010](http://farm6.static.flickr.com/5207/5253354915_1df2096af7_m.jpg)](http://www.flickr.com/photos/cavalleto/5253354915/ "WordCamp Spain 2010 by Cavalleto, on Flickr") This Saturday was held the [WordCamp Spain 2010](http://wordcamp.es/) here in Barcelona, a whole day full of more than interesting talks about [WordPress](http://www.xarop.com/tag/wordpress/) and the world that surrounds it. I was not able to attend all the presentations but what I was able to hear was very interesting: At all times we were reminded that WordPress is not a blog manager, it has always been a [CMS](http://www.xarop.com/tag/cms/) with which we can attack any type of website. [Rafael Poveda](http://raven.es/ "Rafael Poveda") told us about the [themes](http://www.xarop.com/tag/theme/), the visual part of this CMS. In each new version of WordPress, the creation of user interfaces becomes easier even though this platform is more powerful each time. Rafael recommended the use of *Frameworks* or the creation of child themes of already existing themes. *WPcandy* makes us a [comparison between the main WordPress frameworks](http://wpcandy.com/presents/wordpress-theme-framework-comparison), among all of them we will surely find the one that best suits our needs. [Mr. Muñoz](http://www.senormunoz.es/ "Mr. Muñoz") gave us a pleasant talk about SEO and [SEO plugins for WordPress](http://www.senormunoz.es/SEO-MARBELLA/wordcamp-seo-plugins), along the way he responded to a few consulting requests on various websites that he made right there, very instructive. The first talk of the afternoon was somewhat disappointing, [Luis Rull](http://luisrull.com/ "Luis Rull") went off the rails and was not as instructive as the morning speakers, he spoke to us about [WordPress as CMS](http://luisrull.es/2010/12/wordcamp-espana-2010-wordpress-como-cms.html "WordCamp España 2010: WordPress as a CMS") but without mentioning really practical topics on how to get a website comparable to one made with Drupal. Then the guys from Automattic and WhooThemes spoke, as well as WordPress on mobile devices. I was not able to attend the final workshop on plugin creation or the talk on "WP as an e-commerce platform" by [Fernando Serer](http://www.fernandoserer.com/ "Fernando Serer"), but in general I found it to be a very complete and interesting WordCamp, perhaps too much for a single day since I would have liked more space for the intervention of the attendees.
